ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002473 represents a generic chicken sandwich. No exact code exists for a breaded fried chicken fillet sandwich from a fast-food chain. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP is the closest available category for a fast-food chicken sandwich. No exact Jollibee-specific code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for a fast-food fried chicken sandwich product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One Original Chicken Sandwich consisting of a breaded fried chicken fillet in a soft bun with mayonnaise and lettuce, weighing approximately 175 g.

nutrient_source.title: Fast Food, Chicken Fillet Sandwich, Plain — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy item 'Fast Food, Chicken Fillet Sandwich, Plain' (FDC ID 175194) used as the primary nutrient reference. This is the closest approved-source match for a standard fast-food breaded fried chicken sandwich. Values are per 100 g as reported. Jollibee Singapore does not publish full nutrition panels in an approved database; USDA SR Legacy is the highest-priority approved source available for this food category.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal, American Diabetes Association)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Jollibee Original Chicken Sandwich. GI of 66 is derived from the analogous food 'hamburger bun / white wheat bread-based fast-food sandwich' category in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care), which reports GI values of 61–72 for white-bread-based fast-food sandwiches. A midpoint estimate of 66 is used. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (66 × 46.9) / 100 ≈ 31.0 g, where carbohydrate per 175 g serving = 26.8 × 1.75 = 46.9 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~537 mg per 100 g; ~940 mg per sandwich serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this sandwich due to its high sodium content (~940 mg per serving, approximately 40% of the 2,300 mg daily limit), which can elevate blood pressure; occasional consumption should be paired with low-sodium foods throughout the day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate load and medium-to-high glycemic index (GI 66) with a glycemic load of ~31 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this sandwich as its medium GI (66) combined with a high glycemic load (~31 per serving) and refined white-bun carbohydrates can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; portion control and pairing with low-GI foods or non-starchy vegetables is strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (3.0 g/100 g) and presence of trans fat (~0.2 g/100 g) from deep-frying, combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake of this fried chicken sandwich due to its saturated fat, residual trans fat from frying, and high sodium content, all of which are associated with adverse lipid profiles and elevated cardiovascular risk; consumption should be infrequent and portion-controlled.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Original Chicken Sandwich is a fast-food sandwich consisting of a deep-fried, breaded chicken breast fillet served in a soft white burger bun, typically accompanied by mayonnaise and a leaf of lettuce. It is the signature sandwich product of Jollibee, a Filipino fast-food chain with outlets across Southeast Asia including Singapore. The chicken fillet is marinated, coated in a seasoned wheat-flour breading, and deep-fried in vegetable oil, yielding a crispy exterior with a juicy, tender interior. The bun is soft and slightly sweet in the Filipino fast-food tradition. Per 100 g, the sandwich provides approximately 283 kcal, 14.8 g protein, 13.5 g fat, and 26.8 g carbohydrate, with a notably high sodium content of 537 mg. The glycemic index is estimated at 66 (medium), and the glycemic load per standard 175 g serving is approximately 31, making it a high-glycemic-load meal. Clinically, this food is of concern for individuals managing hypertension (high sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (high glycemic load, refined carbohydrates), and cardiovascular disease (saturated fat, trans fat, sodium). It is certified Halal and is a popular lunch and dinner option in Singapore fast-food settings. The meal version (GD000507) is sold at Jollibee Bugis Village for SGD 9.90.
common_user_queries: What are the calories in a Jollibee chicken sandwich?
Is the Jollibee Original Chicken Sandwich halal?
How much sodium is in a chicken sandwich from Jollibee?
Is a fried chicken sandwich good for diabetics?
What is the glycemic index of a fast-food chicken sandwich?
Jollibee chicken sandwich nutrition facts Singapore
Can I eat a chicken sandwich if I have high blood pressure?
How many carbs in a Jollibee chicken sandwich?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy 'Fast Food, Chicken Fillet Sandwich, Plain' (FDC ID 175194), which is the closest approved-source match. Jollibee Singapore does not publish full nutrition data in any approved primary database. GI is an analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact Jollibee-specific nutrient panel from an approved source is available.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 175194 (Fast Food, Chicken Fillet Sandwich, Plain) as no Jollibee-specific nutrition data appears in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA Branded Foods, or other approved primary databases at the time of processing. 2. Serving size of 175 g is estimated based on typical fast-food chicken sandwich weights reported in USDA SR Legacy. 3. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for the Jollibee Original Chicken Sandwich was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food category. 5. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food category. 6. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food category. 7. The food is tagged Halal per Jollibee Singapore's Halal certification (MUIS-certified). 8. trans_fat_g of 0.2 g/100 g reflects residual trans fats from deep-frying in partially hydrogenated or high-temperature vegetable oils as reported in analogous USDA fast-food entries; this value should be verified if Jollibee publishes an official nutrition panel. 9. Price SGD 9.90 is preserved from the field survey data source record (GD000507).
